# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-18
**Delivered:** 11:22 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative this week has been the dramatic reversal in Middle East war risk premium: Iran's declaration that the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to commercial shipping triggered a historic ~9-11% single-day collapse in crude prices, while equity markets surged to fresh record highs. The single most important variable for today's session is the durability of the ceasefire and the status of US-Iran diplomatic talks, which will determine whether the crude sell-off extends or reverses sharply. The S&P 500 notched one of its fastest recoveries in years — from near-correction to all-time closing high in just 54 trading days — powered by resilient bank earnings and a cooler-than-expected PPI print [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html]. Gold remains near multi-month highs as markets balance geopolitical relief against sticky above-target inflation and an on-hold Fed.

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro backdrop has shifted materially in the past 48 hours. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" to commercial vessels during the ceasefire period, triggering a rapid de-risking in energy markets and a simultaneous surge in equities [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-17-2026]. The S&P 500 closed at 7,126.06, up +1.2% on the session, while the Nasdaq 100 rose +1.29% to 26,672.43 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average added +1.79% to 49,447.43 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC, ^NDX, ^DJI)]. The VIX dropped -2.56% to 17.48, reflecting the rapid reduction in near-term volatility expectations [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

On the inflation front, US CPI YoY stands at 3.32% as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL], well above the Fed's 2% target, while the 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.32% and the 2-year at 3.78% as of April 16, producing a 54bp positive slope on the curve [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10, https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS2]. The Federal Funds effective rate stands at 3.64% [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS]. A cooler-than-expected PPI report helped fuel this week's equity rally, though the broader inflation trajectory remains complicated by the energy shock from the Middle East war [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html].

The Federal Reserve remains firmly on hold. Fed Governor Christopher Waller acknowledged the Middle East war is likely to drive near-term inflation higher but left the door open to rate cuts later in 2026 if the conflict resolves swiftly [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/feds-waller-says-swift-war-end-could-keep-rate-cut-hopes-alive]. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack reiterated that rates are "in a good place" and likely on hold "for a good while," given the complexity of an energy price shock layered on top of already-elevated inflation [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-15/feds-hammack-rates-likely-hold-good-while-cnbc]. Deutsche Bank now projects the Fed will hold rates unchanged through all of 2026, citing oil-driven inflation risks, resilient growth, and a tight labor market [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/deutsche-bank-expects-fed-hold-rates-2026-2026-04-17/].

The IMF has cut its global growth forecast in response to the Iran conflict, with Chief Economist Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas warning the war has "halted the positive global momentum" by feeding into higher commodity prices, rising inflation expectations, and tighter financial conditions [Source: https://www.newsweek.com/map-shows-countries-taking-biggest-hit-iran-war-11833834]. The IMF projects the US economy will grow 2.3% in 2026, down 0.1% from its January outlook [Source: https://www.newsweek.com/map-shows-countries-taking-biggest-hit-iran-war-11833834]. The ECB is meanwhile navigating a "layer cake of shocks," with markets pricing at least 50bps of ECB rate hikes by year-end and ECB President Lagarde having signaled readiness to tighten if inflation surprises to the upside [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html].

Goldman Sachs has flagged two-sided risks to its 2026 oil price outlook, maintaining Brent at $83/bbl and WTI at $78/bbl as annual averages, assuming Strait of Hormuz flows normalize by mid-May, while noting that faster-than-expected peace progress could send prices materially lower in the near term [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/goldman-sees-softer-oil-demand-flags-two-sided-risks-2026-price]. The IEA has warned oil and gas infrastructure recovery could take up to two years following war damage, underscoring that supply disruption tail risks remain even if a diplomatic resolution is reached [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/IEA-Chief-Says-Oil-Gas-Recovery-Could-Take-Two-Years-After-War-Damage.html].

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" to commercial vessels for the duration of the current US-Iran ceasefire, triggering a sharp ~9-11% collapse in crude oil prices on April 17 [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-17-2026].
- US General Dan Caine confirmed the US had expanded its naval blockade of Iran to "all ships, regardless of nationality" beyond the Middle East, even as ceasefire diplomacy progressed [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-16-2026].
- President Trump announced Israel and Lebanon reached a 10-day cease-fire agreement, and investors are growing increasingly confident in eventual US-Iran peace talks, though no date or venue for a second round of negotiations has been secured [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-16-2026].
- A Pakistani mediator was reported in Tehran, and the Trump administration is actively talking up the prospects of a deal that would permanently open the Strait of Hormuz [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-16/gold-rises-middle-east-optimism-calms-inflation-fears].
- The IEA warned the Iran conflict could "reshape global energy markets" for years, while NATO allies have declined to participate in the proposed US Strait of Hormuz blockade, creating friction among Western allies [Source: https://www.oilandgas360.com/360-energy-pulse-what-mattered-this-week-in-energy-9/].
- The IMF released parallel adverse and severe recession scenarios tied to a prolonged conflict, underscoring that ceasefire durability is the single most important macro variable globally [Source: https://www.axios.com/2026/04/15/iran-war-imf-world-bank].
- The Nomura bank estimated a full US blockade could cut off another 2.3 million barrels per day of oil supply, highlighting the tail-risk of re-escalation [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/IEA-Chief-Says-Oil-Gas-Recovery-Could-Take-Two-Years-After-War-Damage.html].

**USD/CHF — ~0.7811** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- The CHF is strengthening as USD/CHF fell -0.27%, consistent with reduced geopolitical safe-haven demand for USD and continued Swiss franc appeal as a low-inflation reserve currency [Source: Yahoo Finance (USDCHF=X)]. SNB's negative real rate posture remains a long-term CHF support.

**NZD/USD — ~0.5885** | Bias: Neutral
- NZD is barely changed (-0.02%), trading near multi-month lows on a relative basis but supported by commodity currency tailwinds from the geopolitical reshaping narrative [Source: Yahoo Finance (NZDUSD=X)]. RBNZ's relatively dovish stance caps near-term upside.
- Key levels: 0.5820 support | 0.5960 resistance
- Catalyst: RBNZ OCR decision; NZ CPI; China demand signals

**EUR/JPY — ~186.605** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- EUR/JPY slid -0.48% as the yen found modest support from falling US yields and reduced energy inflation risk [Source: Yahoo Finance (EURJPY=X)]. ECB hawkish rhetoric provides a partial offset, keeping the cross in a tug-of-war.

**GBP/JPY — ~214.411** | Bias: Neutral-Bear

**EUR/GBP — ~0.8703** | Bias: Neutral

- Key levels: 0.8640 support | 0.8780 resistance

### US Equities

**S&P 500 — ~7,126.06** | Bias: Bullish
- The S&P 500 closed at 7,126.06, up +1.2%, with the index posting one of the fastest recoveries on record — from near-correction (-9%) to all-time high in just 54 trading days [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC), https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html]. Semiconductor, media/internet, and hardware stocks are leading the AI-themed rally alongside resilient bank earnings from JPMorgan and Bank of America [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html].

**Nasdaq 100 — ~26,672.43** | Bias: Bullish
- The Nasdaq 100 rose +1.29% to 26,672.43 and the Composite extended its win streak to 11 consecutive days, powered by AI tech buying [Source: Yahoo Finance (^NDX), https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041526]. Netflix results are in focus for the coming sessions, with 83 S&P 500 companies reporting next week [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/these-stocks-with-earnings-next-week-historically-beat-expectations.html].

**Dow Jones — ~49,447.43** | Bias: Bullish
- The Dow surged +1.79% to 49,447.43, led by financial and industrial components benefiting from the Middle East de-escalation and resilient consumer health data from bank earnings [Source: Yahoo Finance (^DJI)]. Seven Dow components report next week, including companies with 70%+ historical earnings beat rates [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/these-stocks-with-earnings-next-week-historically-beat-expectations.html].

### Asia

**Nikkei 225 — ~58,475.90** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- The Nikkei fell -1.75% to 58,475.90, underperforming global peers as yen safe-haven buying (even modest) tends to hurt export-heavy Japanese equities, and the index remains sensitive to USD/JPY dynamics [Source: Yahoo Finance (^N225)]. The Middle East ceasefire optically benefits Japan as a major energy importer, but yen appreciation risk offsets this.

- The KOSPI dipped -0.55% to 6,191.92, reflecting Korea's sensitivity to global semiconductor trade flows and the residual uncertainty around Middle East supply chains affecting technology manufacturing inputs [Source: Yahoo Finance (^KS11)].

- The ASX 200 was nearly flat (-0.09%) at 8,946.90; Australia benefits from commodity currency tailwinds but falling crude prices pose a near-term headwind for energy sector components [Source: Yahoo Finance (^AXJO)]. The IMF's mild downgrade to Australia's 2026 growth adds a modest weight [Source: https://www.theguardian.com/business/grogonomics/2026/apr/15/imf-world-economic-outlook-chaos-donald-trump].

### Europe

**DAX — ~24,702.24** | Bias: Bullish
- The DAX surged +2.27% to 24,702.24, outperforming all major indices, as European equities benefit disproportionately from Hormuz reopening — Germany's energy-intensive industrial base is directly relieved by lower crude prices [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GDAXI)]. The ECB's potential rate hike path remains a medium-term headwind, but near-term sentiment is clearly positive [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html].

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 30% | US-Iran peace talks formalize a second round; ceasefire extended; oil stabilizes; tech earnings beat | $86 | 7,350 | $82,000 |
| Base | 50% | Ceasefire holds but no breakthrough; oil range-bound; earnings season mixed; Fed stays on hold | $90 | 7,100 | $75,000 |
| Bear | 20% | Ceasefire collapses; Hormuz re-blockaded; oil spikes above $100; Fed forced to hike; equities sell off | $105 | 6,600 | $65,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts (Week of April 18–25, 2026)

1. **ECB April Rate Decision** (within ~2 weeks) — Markets pricing ≥50bps of hikes by year-end; the April meeting could be a major inflection point for EUR and European equities [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html]
2. **US Flash PMI Data** (next week) — Manufacturing and services PMIs will reveal the first hard data on how the Iran war energy shock has impacted US business sentiment [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/week-ahead-for-fx-bonds-u-s-retail-sales-pmi-data-due-as-focus-remains-on-middle-east-4b43a748]
3. **US Retail Sales** (next week) — Key gauge of consumer resilience in the face of elevated energy prices and above-target inflation [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/week-ahead-for-fx-bonds-u-s-retail-sales-pmi-data-due-as-focus-remains-on-middle-east-4b43a748]
4. **S&P 500 Q1 Earnings Season Peak** (next week, 83 companies = ~17% of index) — Lam Research, ServiceNow, and 7 Dow components reporting; results will test whether corporate earnings can sustain record equity valuations [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/these-stocks-with-earnings-next-week-historically-beat-expectations.html]
5. **US-Iran Diplomatic Developments / Strait of Hormuz Status** (ongoing) — The single most binary macro variable; any breakdown in ceasefire or progress toward a formal peace deal will dominate all asset classes, especially crude, gold, and JPY [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/reopened-strait-hormuz-falling-oil-prices-may-recast-feds-options-future-cuts-2026-04-17/]

### Disputed

- [The report states the 2-10yr Treasury curve slope is 54bps positive (4.32% - 3.78%)]: The arithmetic is correct (54bps), but the report characterizes this as a "54bp positive slope" without noting the Fed Funds rate (3.64%) is *below* the 2-year (3.78%), suggesting markets are already pricing a modest policy premium — a nuance the report omits but does not technically misstate.
- [The report attributes the Standard Chartered $7,500 ETH price target as sourced from "market commentary"]: The source cited (https://markets.businessinsider.com/news/stocks/crypto-news-pepeto-presale-fast-sell-out-stage-whilst-ethereum-price-prediction-reaches-7-500-by-standard-
